RQ1.title: Music Discovery Friction
RQ1.executive_summary: Users face significant barriers when trying to find and discover new music on Spotify, primarily due to ineffective music recommendations, lack of personalization and control, and technical issues with the app. The algorithm-driven playlists, app performance, and user interface require improvement to address these issues.

jtbd_summary.situation: When looking for new music to listen to
jtbd_summary.motivation: To discover new music that they will enjoy
jtbd_summary.outcome: To find new music that matches their taste and preferences

observed_workarounds: Using a different music streaming service

key_findings.finding: Ineffective music recommendations are a major barrier to music discovery
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster_6: 70% of users reported poor music recommendations, Cluster_172: 60% reported poor weekly discovery recommendations, Cluster_158: 60% reported poor algorithmic recommendations

key_findings.finding: Lack of control over music recommendations is a significant issue
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster_6: 30% of users reported lack of control over recommendations, Cluster_172: 40% reported lack of control over weekly discovery, Cluster_158: 40% reported lack of control over algorithmic recommendations

key_findings.finding: Technical issues with the app, such as poor performance and user interface issues, hinder music discovery
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster_172: users reported issues with the app, such as uninstalling and reinstalling, Cluster_906: users reported issues with playlist updates

actionable_opportunities.proposed_feature: Enhanced algorithm-driven playlists with user-controlled preferences

actionable_opportunities.opportunity: Enhance user control over music recommendations
actionable_opportunities.proposed_feature: User-controlled playlist features, such as manual mixing and playlist editing

actionable_opportunities.opportunity: Optimize app performance and user interface
actionable_opportunities.unmet_need: Seamless and intuitive music discovery experience
actionable_opportunities.proposed_feature: Improved app performance, user-friendly interface, and regular updates

RQ2.title: Algorithmic Repetition & Looping in Automated Mixes
RQ2.executive_summary: Users experience significant repetition in automated mixes like Smart Shuffle and Daily Mixes, with many reporting the same songs being played repeatedly, leading to a poor user experience. The issue is attributed to flaws in the shuffle feature, lack of control over shuffle, and poor algorithmic recommendations.

jtbd_summary.situation: When listening to automated mixes, users want to discover new music and avoid repetition
jtbd_summary.motivation: To enjoy a diverse and engaging music experience without the hassle of manual playlist management
jtbd_summary.outcome: A personalized and dynamic music experience with minimal repetition and maximum discovery

observed_workarounds: Creating a new playlist with specific songs
Using a different music streaming service

key_findings.finding: Users experience significant repetition in automated mixes
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster_14, Cluster_4, Cluster_27, Cluster_537, and Cluster_131

key_findings.finding: Flaws in the shuffle feature and lack of control over shuffle contribute to repetition
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster_14 and Cluster_27

key_findings.finding: Poor algorithmic recommendations lead to a poor user experience
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster_4

actionable_opportunities.opportunity: Improve the shuffle feature to reduce repetition
actionable_opportunities.unmet_need: Users want a more dynamic and diverse music experience
actionable_opportunities.proposed_feature: Enhanced shuffle algorithm with user-controlled parameters

actionable_opportunities.opportunity: Provide more control over automated mixes
actionable_opportunities.unmet_need: Users want to personalize their music experience
actionable_opportunities.proposed_feature: User-controlled filters and preferences for automated mixes

RQ3.title: Spotify Recommendation Algorithm Sentiment Analysis
RQ3.executive_summary: Users exhibit mixed sentiments towards Spotify's recommendation algorithms, with key areas of dissatisfaction including poor music recommendations, lack of control over recommendations, and perceived biases. However, many users appreciate the music discovery features and the ability to find new music that aligns with their tastes.

jtbd_summary.situation: When looking for new music to listen to
jtbd_summary.motivation: To discover new music and artists that fit their tastes
jtbd_summary.outcome: Easily discover new music and artists with an improved algorithm

observed_workarounds: Using a different music streaming service

key_findings.finding: Poor music recommendations are a major source of dissatisfaction
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster 6: 70% of users reported poor music recommendations

key_findings.finding: Lack of control over recommendations is a significant issue
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster 6: 30% of users reported lack of control over recommendations

key_findings.finding: Users want an improved artist and genre algorithm
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster 935: 96% of users reported needing algorithm improvement

actionable_opportunities.proposed_feature: Enhanced algorithm that incorporates user feedback and listening habits

RQ4.title: User Discovery Methods & Behaviors
RQ4.executive_summary: Users employ various approaches to discover new music on Spotify, including manual search, custom playlists, and features like Discover Weekly and AI DJ. However, users often experience issues with shuffle functionality, playlist management, and autoplay, which can hinder their music discovery experience. The evidence suggests that users desire more control over their music listening experience, effective playlist management, and accurate algorithmic recommendations.

jtbd_summary.situation: When users are listening to music on Spotify
jtbd_summary.motivation: They want to discover new music and have control over their music listening experience
jtbd_summary.outcome: So that they can enjoy their music and find new songs that they will like

observed_workarounds: Creating a new playlist with specific songs
Using a different music streaming service

key_findings.finding: Users experience issues with shuffle functionality
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster 14: 60% of users experience shuffle to different playlists, and 40% experience lack of control over shuffle

key_findings.finding: Users want effective playlist management
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster 154: 50% of users experience songs disappearing from playlists, and 50% experience difficulty adding songs to playlists

key_findings.finding: Users want accurate algorithmic recommendations
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster 172: 60% of users experience poor weekly discovery recommendations, and 40% experience lack of control over weekly discovery

actionable_opportunities.unmet_need: Users want more control over their music listening experience
actionable_opportunities.proposed_feature: Allow users to customize shuffle settings and prevent shuffling to different playlists
Implement a more intuitive playlist editing interface and prevent songs from disappearing from playlists
Improve the weekly discovery algorithm to provide more relevant and personalized recommendations

RQ5.title: Feature-Specific Performance of Spotify Discovery Features
RQ5.executive_summary: Spotify users express dissatisfaction with the performance of specific discovery features, including Smart Shuffle, Release Radar, and Discover Weekly, citing issues with unexpected song transitions, duplicate songs, and playlist management, highlighting opportunities for improvement to enhance the overall user experience.

jtbd_summary.situation: Users are listening to music on Spotify using various discovery features
jtbd_summary.motivation: Users want to discover new music, manage their playlists effectively, and listen to songs from the same playlist without issues
jtbd_summary.outcome: Users want to enjoy a seamless music listening experience with accurate recommendations and control over their playlists

observed_workarounds: Creating a new playlist with specific songs
Using a different music streaming service

key_findings.finding: Users experience issues with Smart Shuffle, including unexpected song transitions and lack of control
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster 14, Cluster 27, Cluster 536

key_findings.finding: Users have difficulty managing their playlists, including adding and removing songs
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster 154

key_findings.finding: Users are dissatisfied with the weekly discovery feature, citing poor recommendations and lack of control
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster 172

actionable_opportunities.opportunity: Improve Smart Shuffle algorithm to reduce unexpected song transitions
actionable_opportunities.unmet_need: Users want control over their music listening experience
actionable_opportunities.proposed_feature: Enhanced Smart Shuffle with user-controlled preferences

actionable_opportunities.opportunity: Enhance playlist management features to reduce user frustration
actionable_opportunities.unmet_need: Users want to manage their playlists effectively
actionable_opportunities.proposed_feature: Streamlined playlist editing and song addition/removal process

actionable_opportunities.opportunity: Refine weekly discovery feature to provide more accurate recommendations
actionable_opportunities.unmet_need: Users want to discover new music that they will enjoy
actionable_opportunities.proposed_feature: Personalized weekly discovery feature with user-controlled preferences

RQ6.title: Physical Listening Contexts and Spotify Discovery Features
RQ6.executive_summary: The physical environments in which users listen to Spotify, such as the gym or during commutes, significantly impact the usability and effectiveness of its discovery features. Users seek seamless music experiences tailored to their activities, but current issues like app crashes and slow loading hinder this. Opportunities exist to enhance workout and fitness playlists, improving the overall user experience.

jtbd_summary.situation: Users are engaging in physical activities such as working out or commuting and want to listen to music that enhances their experience
jtbd_summary.motivation: To have a seamless and motivating music experience that complements their physical activity
jtbd_summary.outcome: To find and listen to music that motivates and improves their workouts or commutes

observed_workarounds: Using a different music streaming service
Using playlists from other music streaming services

key_findings.finding: App crashes and slow loading hinder the music listening experience
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster 211: App Crashing (60.0%) and Slow App Loading (40.0%)

key_findings.finding: Users seek music playlists tailored to their workout and fitness activities
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster 689: Workout and Fitness Playlists

key_findings.finding: Current discovery features do not fully meet user needs for workout and fitness playlists
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster 689: Limited Fitness Playlists (0.7%)

actionable_opportunities.unmet_need: Users want music that motivates and improves their workouts
actionable_opportunities.proposed_feature: Personalized workout playlists based on user preferences and activity type

actionable_opportunities.unmet_need: Users want a seamless music listening experience
actionable_opportunities.proposed_feature: Optimize app loading times and reduce crashes

RQ7.title: Monetization & Feature Access Impact on Music Discovery Experience
RQ7.executive_summary: Free-tier restrictions and premium upselling significantly influence users' music discovery experience and overall satisfaction, primarily due to excessive ads, limited song control, and aggressive premium promotion. Users seek a seamless music listening experience with fewer ads and more control over their music experience, driving them to consider premium upgrades or alternative services.

jtbd_summary.situation: Users are listening to music on the free version of a music streaming service
jtbd_summary.motivation: To have a seamless music listening experience with fewer ads and more control over their music experience
jtbd_summary.outcome: To be able to listen to music without interruptions and have more control over their music experience

observed_workarounds: Using a different music streaming service

key_findings.finding: Excessive ads are a major pain point for users on the free tier
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster 167, Cluster 151, and Cluster unrelated_ads all mention too many ads as a significant issue

key_findings.finding: Limited song control is a significant issue for users on the free tier
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster 167 and Cluster 139 mention limited features and lack of control over music experience as significant issues

key_findings.finding: Aggressive premium promotion can be off-putting to users
key_findings.supporting_evidence: Cluster 139 mentions that the app forces users to subscribe to premium

actionable_opportunities.opportunity: Improve the free-tier experience by reducing ads and increasing song control
actionable_opportunities.unmet_need: Users want a seamless music listening experience with fewer ads and more control over their music experience
actionable_opportunities.proposed_feature: Introduce a mid-tier subscription option with fewer ads and more features

actionable_opportunities.opportunity: Enhance premium promotion by highlighting the benefits of upgrading
actionable_opportunities.unmet_need: Users are unsure about the value of upgrading to premium
actionable_opportunities.proposed_feature: Introduce a free trial period for premium features to showcase the benefits of upgrading
